@@ -637,7 +637,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","title":"TASK: Define Data Migration Strategy for Schema Changes","description":" ## Evolving the Data Model Without Losing User Data As the Brenner Loop evolves, the data schema will change: - Adding new fields to HypothesisCard - Restructuring EvidenceEntry - New session metadata Users have existing sessions in LocalStorage. We can't break them. ### Migration Architecture \`\`\`typescript // Version marker in all data interface SessionData { _version: number; // Schema version // ... rest of session } // Migration registry const MIGRATIONS: Record = { 2: migrateV1ToV2, 3: migrateV2ToV3, // etc. }; const CURRENT_VERSION = 3; // Migration function type type Migration = (data: unknown) => unknown; \`\`\` ### Migration Flow \`\`\` Load session from storage │ ▼ Check _version field │ ├── If current → Use directly │ └── If older → Run migrations │ ▼ Run each migration in sequence: v1 → v2 → v3 → current │ ▼ Save migrated data back │ ▼ Continue with session \`\`\` ### Example Migrations \`\`\`typescript // V1 → V2: Add confidence history function migrateV1ToV2(data: SessionV1): SessionV2 { return { ...data, _version: 2, hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({ ...h, confidenceHistory: [{ confidence: h.confidence, timestamp: data.createdAt }] })) }; } // V2 → V3: Add prediction lock timestamp function migrateV2ToV3(data: SessionV2): SessionV3 { return { ...data, _version: 3, hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({ ...h, predictions: h.predictions.map(p => ({ ...p, lockedAt: p.locked ? data.updatedAt : null })) })) }; } \`\`\` ### Migration Safety 1. **Backup before migration**: \`\`\`typescript async function safeLoadSession(id: string): Promise { const raw = localStorage.getItem(\`session:\${id}\`); const data = JSON.parse(raw); if (data._version < CURRENT_VERSION) { // Backup original localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}:backup:\${data._version}\`, raw); // Migrate const migrated = runMigrations(data); localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}\`, JSON.stringify(migrated)); return migrated; } return data; } \`\`\` 2. **Rollback capability**: \`\`\`typescript function rollbackMigration(sessionId: string, toVersion: number): boolean { const backupKey = \`session:\${sessionId}:backup:\${toVersion}\`; const backup = localStorage.getItem(backupKey); if (backup) { localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${sessionId}\`, backup); return true; } return false; } \`\`\` 3. **Version compatibility check**: \`\`\`typescript function isVersionSupported(version: number): boolean { return version >= MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION && version <= CURRENT_VERSION; } \`\`\` ### Testing Migrations \`\`\`typescript describe('Migrations', () => { test('V1 → V2 adds confidence history', () => { const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json'); const v2Data = migrateV1ToV2(v1Data); expect(v2Data._version).toBe(2); expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory).toBeDefined(); expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory.length).toBe(1); }); test('Full migration path V1 → current', () => { const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json'); const currentData = runMigrations(v1Data); expect(currentData._version).toBe(CURRENT_VERSION); expect(validateSession(currentData)).toBe(true); }); }); \`\`\` ### File Locations \`\`\` apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/index.ts apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v1-to-v2.ts apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v2-to-v3.ts apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/__tests__/ \`\`\` ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Version field in all persisted data - [ ] Migration functions for each version bump - [ ] Backup before migration - [ ] Rollback capability - [ ] Migration tests with fixtures - [ ] Handles corrupted data gracefully ### Depends On - 1v26.1 (Session Data Model) - 1v26.2 (LocalStorage Persistence) ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:26:27.571132583-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T00:33:28.794732876-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T00:33:28.794732876-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented session schema migration framework (v0->v1), backups/rollback, tests, and safe persistence","labels":["brenner-loop","data-model","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:27:02.498882631-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:27:05.352437229-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","title":"Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands ## Objective CLI commands for scoring sessions, viewing feedback, and comparing scores. ## Implementation Details ### Commands #### `brenner score ` ```bash brenner score RS-20251230 # Output: Session Score: RS-20251230 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Overall: 72/100 (C) Dimension Breakdown: Paradox Grounding ████████░░ 15/20 (75%) Hypothesis Kill Rate ██████████ 20/20 (100%) ★ Test Discriminability ██████░░░░ 10/15 (67%) Assumption Tracking ████████░░ 12/15 (80%) Third Alternative ████░░░░░░ 4/10 (40%) Experimental Feasibility ██████░░░░ 6/10 (60%) Adversarial Pressure ████░░░░░░ 5/10 (50%) Top Signals: ✓ At least one hypothesis killed (H-RS20251230-001) ✓ Kill linked to test result (T-RS20251230-002) ✗ "Both could be wrong" not considered ✗ Weak adversarial critique Run `brenner feedback RS-20251230` for detailed suggestions. ``` #### `brenner score --all` ```bash brenner score --all # Output (for all sessions in current program): Session Scores ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Session | Score | Grade | Trend ------------------|-------|-------|------- RS-20251230 | 72 | C | - RS-20251231 | 78 | C | ↑ RS-20260101 | 85 | B | ↑ RS-20260102 | 81 | B | ↓ Average: 79 (C) Trend: Improving ↑ Best: RS-20260101 (85, B) Worst: RS-20251230 (72, C) ``` #### `brenner feedback ` ```bash brenner feedback RS-20251230 # Output: Feedback for RS-20251230 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Summary: Session is progressing but has 3 areas to improve. [WARNING] Third Alternative Discovery (4/10) Issue: "Both could be wrong" not explicitly considered Suggestion: Explicitly ask: what if ALL current hypotheses are wrong? What third alternative would explain the data differently? Sydney Brenner says: "Both could be wrong." (§103) [WARNING] Adversarial Pressure (5/10) Issue: Insufficient critique of hypotheses Suggestion: Actively attack your hypotheses. What assumptions are you making? What would a skeptic say? [INFO] Test Discriminability (10/15) Issue: Some tests lack mutually exclusive predictions Suggestion: For each test, ensure hypotheses make DIFFERENT predictions. If H1 and H2 predict the same outcome, the test cannot distinguish them. ``` #### `brenner leaderboard` ```bash brenner leaderboard [--dimension=] [--limit=10] # Output: Brenner Method Leaderboard ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Top Sessions by Total Score: 1. RS-20260101 85 (B) ████████░░ 2. RS-20260102 81 (B) ████████░░ 3. RS-20251231 78 (C) ███████░░░ 4. RS-20251230 72 (C) ███████░░░ Best by Dimension: Paradox Grounding: RS-20260101 (18/20) Hypothesis Kill: RS-20251230, RS-20260101 (20/20 tie) Test Discrimin.: RS-20260102 (14/15) ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Score single session with dimension breakdown - [ ] Score all sessions with aggregation - [ ] Feedback shows prioritized suggestions - [ ] Leaderboard ranks sessions - [ ] Visual progress bars for percentages - [ ] Brenner quotes in feedback - [ ] JSON output for scripting ## Dependencies - Task: Implement Scoring Logic for All Dimensions - Task: Create Aggregation and Grade Functions - Task: Generate Actionable Feedback ## Effort Estimate ~300 lines of code, ~3 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:08.932720705-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:18:36.900715293-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:18:36.900715293-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented brenner score, feedback, and leaderboard commands with full 7-dimension scoring integration","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.742295246-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-yh8l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.960606025-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ls1v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.991501661-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hh4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:57.022966056-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:59.247166603-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-qka","title":"Feature: Mobile Reading Experience","description":"Optimize the experience of reading long-form content on mobile devices. ## Rationale The primary use case for BrennerBot on mobile is READING — users reviewing transcripts, distillations, or quotes. The transcript alone is 40k+ words. This needs to feel effortless. ## User Scenarios 1. Reading through a distillation during a commute (20-45 min) 2. Skimming the transcript to find a remembered quote 3. Comparing sections across documents 4. Reading in bed with dark mode ## Key Optimizations ### Typography - Ensure text doesn't require zooming (min 16px effective) - Optimal line length on narrow screens (45-65 characters) - Proper paragraph spacing for visual breathing room - Consider hyphenation for justified text (optional) ### Navigation Within Documents - Sticky section headers that show current position - Easy jump to section (table of contents) - Progress indicator (already exists, verify it works well) - 'Jump to top' accessible without scrolling up ### Visual Comfort - Proper contrast ratios for outdoor/bright light reading - Dark mode optimized for OLED screens (already have this) - No harsh transitions when scrolling ### Interaction - Smooth scrolling (verify momentum scrolling on iOS) - Section anchors that work on mobile - Easy copy/share of specific quotes ## Deliverables - Typography refinements for mobile - Document navigation improvements - Reading-specific UI enhancements ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Text readable without zooming on 375px width - [ ] Can navigate to any section within 2 taps - [ ] Progress indicator visible during reading - [ ] Dark mode doesn't cause eye strain - [ ] Smooth scrolling on iOS and Android","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:26:32.540075774-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile reading experience complete: sticky section indicator, mobile typography optimizations (16px+ clamp), reading-width constraints (65ch), momentum scrolling for iOS, MobileTocFab with BottomSheet for quick navigation. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ldh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.760040476-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:24.994109586-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","title":"Add demo mode logic to sessions page","description":"# Task: Implement Demo Mode in Sessions List Page ## Context Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e) DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc) ## Current Behavior (src/app/sessions/page.tsx) ```typescript let labLockedReason: string | null = null; if (!labModeEnabled) { labLockedReason = 'Lab mode is disabled. Set BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 to enable orchestration.'; } // ... {labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} ``` When lab mode is disabled, users see a locked state with no useful content. ## Target Behavior When lab mode is disabled: 1. Show DemoSessionsView instead of LockedState 2. Demo sessions link to demo detail pages (handled by brenner_bot-vn19) 3. Keep existing behavior when lab mode IS enabled ## Code Changes Required ### 1. Import demo components and data ```typescript import { DemoSessionsView } from '@/components/sessions/DemoSessionsView'; import { getDemoThreadSummaries } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions'; ``` ### 2. Detect demo mode ```typescript const labModeEnabled = isLabModeEnabled(); const isDemoMode = !labModeEnabled; ``` ### 3. Update conditional rendering ```typescript // Instead of: {labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} // Use: {isDemoMode ? ( ) : labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} ``` ### 4. Adjust header for demo mode When in demo mode, maybe adjust the header to indicate it: ```typescript

Sessions {isDemoMode && (Demo)}

{isDemoMode ? 'Example sessions showing the Brenner Loop research workflow' : 'Browse and monitor Brenner Loop research sessions' }

{/* Keep New Session button - links to /sessions/new which has its own demo handling */}
``` ### 5. Consider the 'Local Sessions' section The page has a 'Local Sessions' section at the bottom that uses ``. This should probably remain visible in demo mode as it's client-side storage. ## Full Integration Flow 1. User visits /sessions on public site (no lab mode) 2. `isDemoMode = true` 3. Render DemoSessionsView showing demo session cards 4. User clicks a demo session card 5. Navigates to /sessions/demo-xxx 6. Detail page detects demo ID, renders demo content (brenner_bot-vn19) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Lab mode enabled: original behavior unchanged (fetches from Agent Mail) - [ ] Lab mode disabled: shows DemoSessionsView with demo sessions - [ ] Header indicates demo mode appropriately - [ ] 'New Session' button still visible and functional - [ ] 'Local Sessions' section still visible - [ ] No console errors - [ ] Works on Vercel preview deployment ## Testing - Local dev: toggle BRENNER_LAB_MODE to verify both paths - `BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 bun run dev` → real mode - `bun run dev` (no var) → demo mode - Vercel preview: verify demo mode works (no lab mode in Vercel env) ## Dependencies - Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc) - DemoSessionsView component (brenner_bot-afui)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:54:38.152954927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:16.565386612-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:16.565386612-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:55:06.801821203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","title":"Add demo mode logic to sessions page","description":"# Task: Implement Demo Mode in Sessions List Page ## Context Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e) DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc) ## Current Behavior (src/app/sessions/page.tsx) ```typescript let labLockedReason: string | null = null; if (!labModeEnabled) { labLockedReason = 'Lab mode is disabled. Set BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 to enable orchestration.'; } // ... {labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} ``` When lab mode is disabled, users see a locked state with no useful content. ## Target Behavior When lab mode is disabled: 1. Show DemoSessionsView instead of LockedState 2. Demo sessions link to demo detail pages (handled by brenner_bot-vn19) 3. Keep existing behavior when lab mode IS enabled ## Code Changes Required ### 1. Import demo components and data ```typescript import { DemoSessionsView } from '@/components/sessions/DemoSessionsView'; import { getDemoThreadSummaries } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions'; ``` ### 2. Detect demo mode ```typescript const labModeEnabled = isLabModeEnabled(); const isDemoMode = !labModeEnabled; ``` ### 3. Update conditional rendering ```typescript // Instead of: {labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} // Use: {isDemoMode ? ( ) : labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} ``` ### 4. Adjust header for demo mode When in demo mode, maybe adjust the header to indicate it: ```typescript

Sessions {isDemoMode && (Demo)}

{isDemoMode ? 'Example sessions showing the Brenner Loop research workflow' : 'Browse and monitor Brenner Loop research sessions' }

{/* Keep New Session button - links to /sessions/new which has its own demo handling */}
``` ### 5. Consider the 'Local Sessions' section The page has a 'Local Sessions' section at the bottom that uses ``. This should probably remain visible in demo mode as it's client-side storage. ## Full Integration Flow 1. User visits /sessions on public site (no lab mode) 2. `isDemoMode = true` 3. Render DemoSessionsView showing demo session cards 4. User clicks a demo session card 5. Navigates to /sessions/demo-xxx 6. Detail page detects demo ID, renders demo content (brenner_bot-vn19) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Lab mode enabled: original behavior unchanged (fetches from Agent Mail) - [ ] Lab mode disabled: shows DemoSessionsView with demo sessions - [ ] Header indicates demo mode appropriately - [ ] 'New Session' button still visible and functional - [ ] 'Local Sessions' section still visible - [ ] No console errors - [ ] Works on Vercel preview deployment ## Testing - Local dev: toggle BRENNER_LAB_MODE to verify both paths - `BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 bun run dev` → real mode - `bun run dev` (no var) → demo mode - Vercel preview: verify demo mode works (no lab mode in Vercel env) ## Dependencies - Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc) - DemoSessionsView component (brenner_bot-afui)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:54:38.152954927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:37.549289722-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:37.549289722-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated DemoSessionsView into sessions list page, shows demo mode when lab mode disabled","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:55:06.801821203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-qtsb","title":"EPIC: The Complete Brenner Loop System","description":"# The Complete Brenner Loop System ## Vision Statement Transform BrennerBot from a corpus browser into a full-featured **research methodology lab** that operationalizes the Brenner approach to discriminative hypothesis testing. ## Background & Rationale The existing BrennerBot provides access to Brenner's primary source material (transcripts, distillations, quote banks) and explains the operators. But the real value of Brenner's methodology lies in **active application**: 1. **Formulating testable hypotheses** with falsifiable predictions 2. **Applying operators systematically** (Level Split, Exclusion Test, Object Transpose, Scale Check) 3. **Running discriminative tests** that could actually rule out possibilities 4. **Updating beliefs based on evidence** in a structured way 5. **Synthesizing insights** through multi-perspective analysis This epic represents the complete transformation from 'documentation about methodology' to 'interactive methodology lab.' ## Core Insight The Brenner approach is fundamentally about **preferring to be ruled out rather than ruled in**. A good hypothesis is one that makes bold predictions that could be wrong. The system should guide users toward thinking that way, not just explain the concepts. ## Success Criteria - Users can run complete research sessions from hypothesis to conclusion - The system guides users toward better (more discriminative) thinking - Multi-agent synthesis provides genuine adversarial pressure - All work products are durable artifacts with clear audit trails - The experience is addictive because it genuinely improves thinking ## Key Dependencies - Existing corpus infrastructure (complete) - Tutorial system (in progress) - Agent Mail integration (foundation exists) - GA4 analytics (complete) ## Technical Constraints - No vendor AI APIs in web app - coordinate via Agent Mail + external agents - Must work gracefully when agents unavailable (degraded but functional) - Must support offline-first for reading/reviewing (PWA future) ## Sub-Components (Each an Epic) 1. Hypothesis Engine - Core data models, cards, evolution tracking 2. Operator Application System - Interactive guided sessions for each operator 3. Multi-Agent Synthesis - Devil's Advocate, Experiment Designer, Brenner Channeler 4. Evidence Ledger - Test tracking, result recording, belief updates 5. Artifact Generation - Research Briefs, exports, structured outputs 6. Semantic Search & Intelligence - Vector search, confound detection 7. Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, state machines, progress 8. Infrastructure & API - Persistence, real-time, collaboration foundation ## Out of Scope for v1 - Multi-user real-time collaboration (deferred to v2) - Public API (deferred to v2) - Mobile native apps (web PWA sufficient for v1) ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:21:45.532587849-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T10:17:01.997405925-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T10:17:01.997405925-05:00","close_reason":"All 8 sub-component epics completed: 1. Hypothesis Engine (an1n) - HypothesisCard, lifecycle, templates, arena, history 2. Operator Application (vw6p) - Level Split, Exclusion Test, Object Transpose, Scale Check 3. Multi-Agent Synthesis (xlk2) - Tribunal, Devil's Advocate, debate, objections 4. Evidence Ledger (njjo) - Evidence entries, confidence updates, prediction locks 5. Artifact Generation (nu8g) - Research briefs, multi-format export, citations 6. Semantic Search (ukd1) - Embeddings, similarity search, quote matching 7. Session Experience (reew) - Context, state machine, phase transitions, coach 8. Infrastructure (1v26) - Comprehensive storage layer, persistence, analytics Success criteria met: - Users can run complete research sessions ✓ - System guides toward discriminative thinking ✓ - Multi-agent synthesis provides adversarial pressure ✓ - Durable artifacts with audit trails ✓ - 70+ TypeScript files implementing the vision ✓ The Complete Brenner Loop System is operational.","labels":["brenner-loop","vision"]} {"id":"brenner_bot-qzap","title":"Test storage layer operations","description":"Test storage read/write/update operations. Focus on: (1) Session CRUD in storage.ts, (2) Hypothesis storage operations, (3) Error handling for corrupt/missing data. Files: storage.ts (71.22%), hypothesis-storage.ts (branches 74.57%), test-storage.ts (branches 73.41%). These are close to threshold - may need only 5-10 additional tests for edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-05T12:46:37.403996103-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T14:31:11.919206383-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T14:31:11.919206383-05:00","close_reason":"Storage tests comprehensive: 279 tests across 10 files (611 assertions). Fixed program-storage.test.ts describe.sequential → describe. Covered: Session CRUD, hypothesis storage, error handling for corrupt/missing data. Files: storage.ts (36 tests), hypothesis-storage.ts, test-storage.ts, all domain storage files.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qzap","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-momc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T12:46:56.973536231-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-qzr","title":"Web orchestration: Windows absolute projectKey should trigger ensure_project","description":"## Problem `apps/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts` currently decides whether to call Agent Mail `ensure_project` using a POSIX-only check: - `projectKey.startsWith("/")` This skips `ensure_project` for Windows absolute paths (e.g. `C:\\repo` or `\\\\server\\share`), causing downstream Agent Mail calls to fail or behave inconsistently. ## Root cause We’re conflating “absolute path” with “starts with /”. ## Fix Use `isAbsolute(projectKey)` from `node:path` (cross-platform) for the decision. ## Acceptance - Windows absolute project keys call `ensure_project`. - Existing POSIX behavior unchanged. - Web tests still pass (`cd apps/web && bun run test`). ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:03:36.022060056-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:07:00.770595997-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:07:00.770595997-05:00","close_reason":"Use node:path isAbsolute(projectKey) for ensure_project gating (Windows-safe); apps/web tests pass","labels":["agent-mail","orchestration","web","windows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qzr","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:03:36.048932296-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} @@ -701,7 +701,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-vdpi","title":"TASK: System Health & Agent Performance Analytics","description":"Track system health beyond user analytics: Agent response times, error rates, Agent Mail latency, session load times, popular operators usage, system resource utilization. Internal modules (parsers, state management, utilities) - DOM rendering via happy-dom - React hooks and components - Data transformations and business logic **✅ ACCEPTABLE to mock/stub:** - `framer-motion` - Animation timing causes test flakiness - External network calls in **unit tests** (not E2E) - for isolation - Browser APIs not available in test env (IntersectionObserver, etc.) **❌ DO NOT mock:** - Internal module dependencies (import real modules) - Component children (render real children) - State management (use real stores) # Current State (2025-12-30) - **Unit tests**: 41 files, 50% coverage threshold - **E2E tests**: 5 spec files - **Logging**: Excellent infrastructure exists # Critical Gaps **Unit Tests Missing:** - 16+ UI components (SpotlightSearch, DistillationViewer, SessionForm, nav, etc.) - Library modules (corpus.ts, prompt-builder.ts, search/*) **E2E Gaps:** - Search workflow (MAIN USER JOURNEY - untested!) - Session lifecycle, error handling, auth flows # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] 80% line coverage (up from 50%) - [ ] All interactive components tested with real DOM - [ ] All critical user journeys have E2E coverage - Coverage thresholds enforced, real implementations used, CI integration complete.","labels":["testing quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:55.792252655-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-vn19","title":"Add demo mode to session detail page","description":"# Task: Demo Mode for Session Detail Page ## Context Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e) DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc) ## CRITICAL PROBLEM Currently, when lab mode is disabled: 1. Sessions LIST page shows LockedState → we're fixing this with DemoSessionsView 2. But session DETAIL page ALSO shows LockedState! From `src/app/sessions/[threadId]/page.tsx` line 319: ```typescript if (!isLabModeEnabled()) { return ; } ``` So when a user clicks a demo session card, they navigate to `/sessions/demo-bio-nanochat-001` and see... a locked page! This breaks the demo experience. ## Solution Overview 1. Detect demo sessions by threadId prefix 2. Load demo messages from fixtures 3. Render session content with demo banner 4. Handle interactive elements appropriately ## Code Changes Required ### 1. Import demo utilities ```typescript import { isDemoSession, getDemoThreadMessages, type DemoMessage } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions'; ``` ### 2. Handle demo sessions BEFORE lab mode check ```typescript export default async function SessionDetailPage({ params }) { const { threadId } = await params; // Handle local sessions (existing behavior) if (threadId.startsWith(LOCAL_SESSION_PREFIX)) { return ; } // *** NEW: Handle demo sessions *** if (isDemoSession(threadId)) { const demoMessages = getDemoThreadMessages(threadId); if (!demoMessages) { return ; } return ; } // THEN do normal lab mode check for real sessions if (!isLabModeEnabled()) { return ; } // ... rest of existing logic } ``` ### 3. Create DemoSessionDetail component Either refactor existing rendering into a shared component, or create a dedicated demo version that: ```typescript function DemoSessionDetail({ threadId, messages }: { threadId: string; messages: DemoMessage[] }) { // Convert DemoMessage[] to AgentMailMessage[] shape for existing components const agentMailMessages = messages.map(m => ({ ...m, // Add any missing fields with defaults })); // Compute status using existing function const status = computeThreadStatus(agentMailMessages); return (
{/* Demo Banner - PROMINENT */} {/* Header */}

Session

{threadId}
{/* NO RefreshControls in demo mode */} ← Back to Sessions
{/* Round Indicator - use computed status */} {/* Status Section */} {/* NO SessionActions in demo mode - or show disabled state */} {/* Compiled Artifact - if exists */} {status.latestArtifact && } {/* Thread Timeline - render messages */}
); } ``` ### 4. Handle Interactive Elements in Demo Mode #### SessionActions Component The `` component has buttons for: - Compile artifact - Request critique - Post delta - Run experiment **In demo mode, these should NOT be shown** because they call API endpoints that require lab mode. Options: A) **Hide completely** (simplest) B) **Show disabled state with tooltip** explaining demo mode C) **Show placeholder** with explanation of what each action does Recommended: **Option A or C** - either hide or show educational placeholder: ```tsx {isDemoMode ? ( ) : ( )} ``` #### RefreshControls Component The `` component polls for real-time updates. **In demo mode, this is unnecessary** and might cause console errors. **Action**: Do not render RefreshControls in demo mode. #### Sub-page Links The header has links to: - Evidence Pack (`/sessions/{id}/evidence`) - Test Queue (`/sessions/{id}/test-queue`) **In demo mode**: Keep the links - they'll navigate to demo preview pages (handled by brenner_bot-tyyh) ### 5. Demo Banner Component ```tsx function DemoBanner() { return (

Demo Session

This is example data demonstrating a Brenner Loop research session. Actions like Compile and Critique are disabled in demo mode.

← All Demo Sessions Set Up Locally →
); } ``` ### 6. DemoSessionNotFound Component For invalid demo session IDs: ```tsx function DemoSessionNotFound({ threadId }: { threadId: string }) { return (

Demo Session Not Found

The demo session "{threadId}" doesn't exist.

View Available Demo Sessions
); } ``` ## Implementation Approach Options ### Option A: Refactor into shared rendering (Recommended) Extract the main rendering logic into components that work with both real and demo data: ``` SessionDetailPage ├── DemoSessionDetail (isDemoMode=true) │ └── SessionDetailContent (messages, isDemoMode=true) └── RealSessionDetail (isDemoMode=false) └── SessionDetailContent (messages, isDemoMode=false) ``` ### Option B: Separate demo component Create a self-contained `DemoSessionDetail` that duplicates some rendering but is simpler to implement. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clicking demo session card navigates to detail page (not locked) - [ ] Demo detail page renders thread messages correctly - [ ] Demo banner prominently displayed - [ ] Session status/phase computed and displayed correctly - [ ] All message types render (KICKOFF, DELTA, COMPILED, CRITIQUE) - [ ] SessionActions hidden or disabled in demo mode - [ ] RefreshControls not rendered in demo mode - [ ] Sub-page links present (Evidence Pack, Test Queue) - [ ] 'Back to Sessions' link works - [ ] Invalid demo ID shows friendly error - [ ] No console errors - [ ] Works on Vercel deployment ## Dependencies - Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc) - need the messages - Coordinates with: Sub-pages task (brenner_bot-tyyh)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:18.766509438-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:09:55.301337904-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vn19","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:29.492802266-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-vn19","title":"Add demo mode to session detail page","description":"# Task: Demo Mode for Session Detail Page ## Context Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e) DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc) ## CRITICAL PROBLEM Currently, when lab mode is disabled: 1. Coordinates with: Sub-pages task (brenner_bot-tyyh)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:18.766509438-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:33.775381868-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vn19","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:29.492802266-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-voa","title":"TanStack Query: Migrate Document Viewers to Query Hooks","description":"# Goal Migrate TranscriptViewer and DistillationViewer to use the new Query hooks instead of receiving data as props from Server Components. ## Background Currently, document viewers receive pre-fetched data as props: ```typescript // TranscriptViewer.tsx interface TranscriptViewerProps { data: TranscriptData; // Passed from Server Component } ``` This works but means: 1. Data is re-fetched on every navigation 2. No caching between page visits 3. Server must parse on every request ## Migration Strategy ### Option A: Keep Server Fetch + Hydrate (Recommended) Server Component prefetches, passes to client via HydrationBoundary. - Best for SEO (content in initial HTML) - Best for performance (no client-side loading flash) - Query still provides caching for subsequent navigations ### Option B: Full Client-Side Fetch Remove server prefetch, let Query handle everything. - Simpler code - Shows loading state on first visit - Worse SEO **We choose Option A** because the corpus pages are content-heavy and benefit from SSR. ## Implementation ### 1. Update corpus/[doc]/page.tsx ```typescript import { HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, dehydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query'; export default async function CorpusDocPage({ params }) { const queryClient = new QueryClient(); await queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['corpus', 'doc', params.doc], queryFn: () => readCorpusDoc(params.doc), }); return ( ); } ``` ### 2. Update TranscriptViewer ```typescript export function TranscriptViewer({ docId }: { docId: string }) { const { data, isLoading, error } = useCorpusDoc(docId); if (isLoading) return ; if (error) return ; // ... rest of component } ``` ### 3. Update DistillationViewer similarly ## Considerations ### Skeleton Components Create loading skeletons that match the layout: - TranscriptSkeleton: TOC + content placeholders - DistillationSkeleton: Header + section placeholders ### Error Boundaries Create error UI for: - Document not found - Parse failure - Network error ### Backward Compatibility During migration, support both patterns: - Accept data as prop (old pattern) - Use Query if docId provided (new pattern) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Query hook - [ ] DistillationViewer uses Query hook - [ ] SSR still works (content in initial HTML) - [ ] Navigation between docs is instant (cached) - [ ] Loading states are graceful - [ ] Error states are handled ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationViewer.tsx ## Files to Create - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptSkeleton.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationSkeleton.tsx","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:54.657062985-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","close_reason":"Migrated document viewers to TanStack Query hooks with HydrationBoundary for SSR prefetching and navigation caching. Created DocumentContentClient.tsx client component that uses useCorpusDoc hook.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-voa","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1r5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:17.466921916-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-vtl8","title":"TASK: Agent Quota & Usage Limits Definition","description":"Define resource limits for sustainability.
